title: PROP-EPNS-004 (SaveFailedState classification) should be required: true given data-loss risk
description: PROP-EPNS-004 verifies that classifyCurrentSession(SaveFailedState) returns { kind: 'dirty' }. This is a high-risk invariant: if SaveFailedState is misclassified (e.g., as 'empty'), the unsaved content would be silently discarded, causing data loss. Despite this risk, PROP-EPNS-004 is marked required: false. The justification for required: true on PROP-EPNS-003 ('misclassification would either cause data loss') applies identically here. The verification architecture document (line 87) does not provide rationale for why this obligation is optional.

evidence.snippet: | PROP-EPNS-004 | classifyCurrentSession(SaveFailedState) always returns { kind: 'dirty', noteId, note } regardless of pendingNextNoteId value | REQ-EPNS-006, REQ-EPNS-007 | 1 | false |

recommendation: Change PROP-EPNS-004 to required: true with the same data-loss rationale used for PROP-EPNS-003.
